title: Newtype USA September and October 2007 article transcript
display_title: Newtype USA September and October 2007 article transcript
wikitext:
[]: Newtype USA September & October 2007 article transcript             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 14, 2010 11:42 pm These were a long time coming and I never got around to them until now. Enjoy? For those not in the know, these were the previous ones in the forums: Newtype USA December 2006 , Newtype USA February 2007 , Newtype USA March 2007 . If I somehow missed including an article, I'll add it to this thread later. The image for Sep 2007--for pages 58 and 59--has Eva bandai figures of Eva-00 and 01 against a backdrop of the moon and forest for Operation Yashima. Pages 60 and 61 include various Eva products/figures as well as more information on 1.0 (included in transcript): Sadamoto's manga vol 11, Shinji Ikari Raising Project (Japanese release) Volume 3, Die Strene Version 2.0, Petite Eva secret figure of Jet Alone (female), UCC canned coffee of Rei alone and Ritsuko with Gendo, 1.0 movie collector's tickets, and Bandai models ("High Complete Progressive" series) of Eva-00 and Eva-01 based on 1.0 reference/production drawings of the Evas. Also, the basic info blurb on the corner of the page says (among general information about the staff and a brief summary of 1.0): "Four new films currently under development. First film scheduled for September 2007 Release in Japan. Second film scheduled for 2008 release in Japan." but old information is old. September 2007 Newtype USA wrote: Visual Crisis As the heat of summer fades, autumn ushers in a wave of cool new anime in Japan. Check out our picks for the most powerful and eye-catching shows of the new season! New Colors The Eva units are sporting new color schemes, but according to mechanical designer Ikuto Yamashita, they're actually not new at all: "These are the colors I used over ten years ago when I first drew the Evas." Other color schemes have changed as well. We can't wait to see where! Worth a thousand words Evangelion unit 01: Shinji Ikari pilots the unit 01 . The continued existence of humanity depends on his actions over the next few hours. A brief moment in time The moon is shining. Shinji Ikari sits alone in the cockpit of Evangelion unit 01 and waits. NERV, the secret military agency he works for, has been steadily losing ground against the terrifying Angels. Operation Yashima, the brainchild of his co-worker Major ( sic: Should be Lt Col) Misato Katsuragi, has only a minuscule probability of turning the tides--but there is no other path left for them to take. Everything rides on this one last desperate gamble, and while Shinji doesn't know it, he holds the fate of humanity in the palm of his hand. Shinji came to NERV to meet his estranged father, but his hopes of a reconciliation were utterly betrayed. His father, the commander of NERV, spared the boy nary a single kind word, immediately forcing the ex-schoolboy into the cockpit of the unit 01 and out into the battlefield. The military is a radically new environment for Shinji. It's not just a novel experience; he's facing death on a daily basis. Coping with the reality of his new life is more than any 14-year-old could be expected to bear, and Shinji is on the brink of collapse. The only thing that saves his mind from utter ruin is the pilot of unit 00--a girl, also 14, named Rei Ayanami. Expressions hardly ever cross Rei's impassive face. She never disobeys orders and has no concern for her own safety, attempting to pilot her Eva unit even when grievously injured. If she had an aura, it'd probably be a chilly, crystalline blue light. No one knows what goes inside her head as she mechanically pilots her Evangelion unit 00. Shinji thinks of her as he waits for the signal to engage the enemy. Time presses on. The night is quiet and deep. Only the cold, cruel light of the moon pierces the gloom. It's hard to believe the still forest will soon be a battleground. The curtain is about to rise on a massive military operation that will consume the whole of Japan, and Shinji is the keystone. Evangelion unit 00: The unit 00 , piloted by Rei Ayanami, will play a critical role in Operation Yashima. Shown here are Bandai's plastic models of Unit 00 and Unit 01. (See page 60 for more info.) The Eva has landed *Colored production drawings of Eva-00* Rei Ayanami's Unit 00 gets a new color scheme--the one originally planned for it over 10 years ago, before the TV series even began. *Sketches of the progressive knife and Eva-01 shoulder pods* The Progressive Knife was redesigned for the new movies, as was the shoulder pods on Unit 01 that hold it. "It's crammed with so many new ideas!" Ikuto Yamashita (Mechanical designer) Ikuto Yamashita was born in Gifu prefecture in 1965. Besides his legendary work on Evangelion , he has also done mechanical designs for shows like Yukikaze and Gigantic Formula , as well as created the manga Dark Whisper . A mountain of new designs! Ikuto Yamashita is the man responsible for taking the giant-robot concept and giving it a radical organic treatment in Evangelion . Now he's taking the opportunity of the new theatrical release to remake many of his core designs from the Eva series. Yamashita first heard news of the new movies from director Hideaki Anno, but at the time, he didn't realize what a monumental task was in front of him. "Anno only mentioned it very briefly in passing, at the very end of a presentation about a bunch of new projects.", he says. "I'd already heard that we were going to be collecting material from the TV series to reuse in a movie, so the announcement didn't surprise me much at all. The real shock came later, once I was already wrapped up in the production." Yep, you guessed it--Yamashita was asked to completely rework many of the key designs from the show. "But it's probably more accurate to say that we returned to our original designs.", he adds. "Back when we were doing the TV series, the production was so tight that we had to drop all the fine details that might cause animation errors or delays. This time, the director's letting us do it the right way from the beginning." As you may have guessed, Yamashita's influence is all over the look of the new movie, from the color schemes of the Eva units to their new weapon configurations--even the new NERV logo. In fact, the entire Eva arsenal (including the Positron Rifle, Progressive Knife and Shield) has been completely redesigned. "When we were doing the TV series, the director was really into the idea of the Eva equipment being actual real-life weapons, scaled up to Eva-size," Yamashita notes. "This time he's basically saying 'Just make it different from what we used before,' so that's what I'm doing with my redesign."What excited Yamashita the most about working on the Rebuild of Evangelion ? "How it's crammed with so many new ideas!" he sounded half-exhausted, but there was plenty of pride in his voice--we can't wait to see his work for ourselves.

[]:             Quote Post by visionfactory » Sun Aug 15, 2010 9:26 pm I'm confused is it about Evangelion Q or Evangelion 2.22?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Aug 15, 2010 9:34 pm visionfactory wrote: I'm confused is it about Evangelion Q or Evangelion 2.22?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 15, 2010 9:44 pm This is about Eva 1.0 considering the date (2007), I just figured it was interesting to up put this information for reference.

[]:             Quote Post by visionfactory » Tue Aug 17, 2010 10:59 pm ok, thanks ^^

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jun 21, 2012 3:28 pm Article scans for reference/archive's sake: Newtype USA September 2007 Volume 06, Number 09 [URL=http://s74.photobucket.com/albums/i267/sailorstardust/Rebuild%20stuff/Newtype%20USA%20Eva/?action=view&current=NTSept07-02.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://s74.photobucket.com/albums/i267/sailorstardust/Rebuild%20stuff/Newtype%20USA%20Eva/?action=view&current=NTSept07-01.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://s74.photobucket.com/albums/i267/sailorstardust/Rebuild%20stuff/Newtype%20USA%20Eva/?action=view&current=NTSept07-04.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://s74.photobucket.com/albums/i267/sailorstardust/Rebuild%20stuff/Newtype%20USA%20Eva/?action=view&current=NTSept07-03.jpg] [/URL] Sorry about the binding issues!
